2603.19715.title: Stepwise: Neuro-Symbolic Proof Search for Automated Systems Verification
2603.19715.paper_summary: Formal verification via interactive theorem proving is increasingly used to ensure the correctness of critical systems, yet constructing large proof scripts remains highly manual and limits scalability. Advances in large language models (LLMs), especially in mathematical reasoning, make their integration into software verification increasingly promising. This paper introduces a neuro-symbolic proof generation framework designed to automate proof search for systems-level verification projects. The framework performs a best-first tree search over proof states, repeatedly querying an LLM for the next candidate proof step. On the neural side, we fine-tune LLMs using datasets of proof state-step pairs; on the symbolic side, we incorporate a range of ITP tools to repair rejected steps, filter and rank proof states, and automatically discharge subgoals when search progress stalls. This synergy enables data-efficient LLM adaptation and semantics-informed pruning of the search space. We implement the framework on a new Isabelle REPL that exposes fine-grained proof states and automation tools, and evaluate it on the FVEL seL4 benchmark and additional Isabelle developments. On seL4, the system proves up to 77.6\% of the theorems, substantially surpassing previous LLM-based approaches and standalone Sledgehammer, while solving significantly more multi-step proofs. Results across further benchmarks demonstrate strong generalization, indicating a viable path toward scalable automated software verification.
2603.19715.paper_summary_zh: 正式驗證透過互動定理證明越來越多地被用來確保關鍵系統的正確性，但構建大型證明腳本仍然高度手動，限制了可擴展性。大型語言模型（LLMs）的進步，特別是在數學推理方面，使其在軟體驗證中的整合越來越有前景。本文介紹了一個神經符號證明生成框架，旨在自動化系統層級驗證項目的證明搜索。該框架在證明狀態上執行最佳優先樹搜索，重複查詢LLM以獲得下一個候選證明步驟。在神經方面，我們使用證明狀態-步驟對的數據集對LLM進行微調；在符號方面，我們整合了一系列ITP工具來修復被拒絕的步驟、過濾和排名證明狀態，並在搜索進展停滯時自動釋放子目標。這種協同作用使得數據高效的LLM適應和語義驅動的搜索空間修剪成為可能。我們在一個新的Isabelle REPL上實現了該框架，該REPL暴露了細粒度的證明狀態和自動化工具，並在FVEL seL4基準測試和其他Isabelle開發中對其進行了評估。在seL4上，該系統證明了高達77.6\%的定理，顯著超越了之前基於LLM的方法和獨立的Sledgehammer，同時解決了顯著更多的多步證明。進一步基準測試的結果顯示出強大的泛化能力，顯示出通往可擴展自動化軟體驗證的可行路徑。
2603.19715.author: Baoding He et.al.
2603.19715.authors: Baoding He, Zenan Li, Wei Sun, Yuan Yao, Taolue Chen, Xiaoxing Ma, Zhendong Su
